summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Null hypothesis (H₀)'?

"Null hypothesis (H₀)" means: The claim of no effect, no difference, or no change; the default assumption we test against using sample data. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Alternative hypothesis (Hₐ)'?

"Alternative hypothesis (Hₐ)" means: The claim we suspect is true and are trying to find evidence for; can be one-sided () or two-sided (≠). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'P-value'?

"P-value" means: The probability of obtaining a test statistic as extreme as or more extreme than the observed value, assuming H₀ is true. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Significance level (α)'?

"Significance level (α)" means: The threshold for rejecting H₀, typically 0.05; if p-value ≤ α, we reject H₀ in favor of Hₐ. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Statistical significance'?

"Statistical significance" means: A result is statistically significant at level α if the p-value is less than or equal to α, providing evidence against H₀. The other options describe different concepts.
